ref: Segregation and recombination shuffle variation back and forth between the two pools with each generation.

basic.text_prompt: A segregáció és a rekombináció a két halmaz közötti váltakozó átmenetek, melyek minden generációban változnak.
basic.m_audio_prompt: I'm not sure what you mean by "Girlich Ford" and "Majuro". Can you give me more context or clarify your question?

formal.text_prompt: A segregáció és a rekombináció a két halmaz közötti váltakozó változásokat és átmeneteket jelentenek generációkban.
formal.m_audio_prompt: Segregation is the process where homologous chromosomes separate during meiosis, resulting in the distribution of genetic material to daughter cells. Recombination, also known as crossing over, is when segments of DNA from different chromosomes exchange places. Shuffle variation refers to the random assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes during meiosis. Back and forth between the two pools with each generation means that the genetic material from both parents is constantly being shuffled and combined in different ways in each new generation.

informal.text_prompt: A szöveg a következőt mondja: "Segregáció és rekombináció: a két halmaz közötti váltakozó változások, kihúzások, átrendezések, különböző generációkban." Ha további kérdésekkel vagy segítséggel van szükséged, jelenjelje meg.
informal.m_audio_prompt: No, it doesn't. Segregation and recombination are processes that occur during meiosis in eukaryotic cells, not in prokaryotic cells like bacteria. basic.text_prompt: Néhány atom instabil nukleusú, ami azt jelenti, hogy kevés vagy egyáltalán nincs szükségük kis vagy semmilyen nyomásra a felbomlásához.
basic.m_audio_prompt: Well, there are a few ways. One way is through nuclear fusion. In nuclear fusion, two or more atomic nuclei combine to form a heavier nucleus. This process releases a lot of energy. Another way is nuclear fission. In nuclear fission, a heavy nucleus splits into two or more lighter nuclei. This also releases a great deal of energy. There are other processes too, like beta decay, but those are a bit more complex. So, yeah, those are some of the main ways. If you want to know more about any of these, just let me know.

formal.text_prompt: Néhány atom instabil nukleusú, ami azt jelenti, hogy kevés vagy egyáltalán nincs szükségük kis vagy semmilyen nyomásra, hogy felbukkanjanak.
formal.m_audio_prompt: Well, when an atom has an unstable nucleus, it can undergo nuclear decay. This can happen through different processes like alpha decay, beta decay, or gamma decay. In alpha decay, the nucleus emits an alpha particle, which is a helium nucleus. In beta decay, a neutron in the nucleus turns into a proton and an electron is emitted. Gamma decay doesn't change the nucleus but just releases energy in the form of gamma rays. So, these are the major processes that can happen to an unstable nucleus. If you want to know more about any specific type of decay, just let me know.

basic.m_audio_prompt: Well, there are a few ways. One way is through nuclear fusion. In nuclear fusion, two or more atomic nuclei combine to form a heavier nucleus. This process releases a lot of energy. Another way is nuclear fission. In nuclear fission, a heavy nucleus splits into two or more lighter nuclei. This also releases a great deal of energy. There are other processes too, like beta decay, but those are a bit more complex. So, yeah, those are some of the main ways. If you want to know more about any of these, just let me know.

formal.text_prompt: Néhány atomnak instabil nukleusai vannak, ami azt jelenti, hogy könnyen bomlhatnak le, ha csak kicsit nyomják őket.
formal.m_audio_prompt: Well, when an atom has an unstable nucleus, it can undergo radioactive decay. This can happen through different processes like alpha decay, beta decay, or gamma decay. Alpha decay involves the emission of an alpha particle, which is a helium nucleus. Beta decay can be of two types - beta - minus decay, where a neutron in the nucleus turns into a proton and an electron, and beta - plus decay, where a proton turns into a neutron and an electron is emitted. Gamma decay doesn't change the nucleus itself but just releases high - energy photons. So, in short, the unstable nucleus can break apart in these ways. If you want to know more about how these processes work in detail, just let me know.
